title: Particle: lai
content:
Particle: lai (लाई)
Role: dative / recipient / goal marker
Transaction direction: money_out_or_goods_out_to_recipient
Direction vector: OUTBOUND
Signal strength: HIGH
Variants: lai, laii, laai, le, lāī
Disambiguation:
  • lai + diye/deko = outgoing payment or goods
  • lai + becheko = sale (credit possible if no payment verb follows)
  • lai + tireko = definite payment out
  • lai before person = recipient; lai before item = object marker (colloquial)
  • X lai Y bhayo = Y happened to X (not a transfer)
Confusions:
  • lai vs le: 'Ram lai' = to Ram (recipient); 'Ram le' = by Ram (agent)
  • colloquially lai sometimes dropped: 'Ram deko' still means 'gave to Ram'
  • lai after non-person (pasal lai) = for the shop
Patterns:
- X lai Y diye → gave Y to X [payment_out_or_credit_given]
- X lai tireko → paid to X [payment_out]
- X lai becheko → sold to X [sale_to_named_party]
- X lai pathaaeko → sent to X [dispatch_out]
- X lai udharo deko → gave credit to X [credit_extended]

disambiguation_rules: lai + diye/deko = outgoing payment or goods
lai + becheko = sale (credit possible if no payment verb follows)
lai + tireko = definite payment out
lai before person = recipient; lai before item = object marker (colloquial)
X lai Y bhayo = Y happened to X (not a transfer)
common_confusions: lai vs le: 'Ram lai' = to Ram (recipient); 'Ram le' = by Ram (agent)
colloquially lai sometimes dropped: 'Ram deko' still means 'gave to Ram'
lai after non-person (pasal lai) = for the shop

title: Particle: le
content:
Particle: le (ले)
Role: ergative / agent / instrument marker
Transaction direction: identifies_acting_party_or_instrument_of_payment
Direction vector: AGENT_IDENTIFIER
Signal strength: HIGH
Variants: le, ley, lee, lé
Disambiguation:
  • le + action verb = agent who performed action
  • instrument le = payment method (cash le, cheque le, online le)
  • le identifies the payer/seller/giver — not the receiver
  • le vs lai: 'Ram le deko' = Ram gave; 'Ram lai deko' = gave to Ram
Confusions:
  • le often dropped in speech: 'Ram tiryo' = 'Ram le tiryo'
  • le after instrument mistaken for agent: 'card le' = by card not 'card paid'
  • colloquial merger: 'usle' = 'usley' (he/she did it)
Patterns:
- X le tiryo → X paid (X is the payer) [payment_by_named_agent]
- X le kinyo → X bought [purchase_by_named_agent]
- cash le tireko → paid by cash [cash_payment_instrument]
- cheque le pathaaeko → sent by cheque [cheque_payment]
- X le becheko → X sold [sale_by_named_agent]

disambiguation_rules: le + action verb = agent who performed action
instrument le = payment method (cash le, cheque le, online le)
le identifies the payer/seller/giver — not the receiver
le vs lai: 'Ram le deko' = Ram gave; 'Ram lai deko' = gave to Ram
common_confusions: le often dropped in speech: 'Ram tiryo' = 'Ram le tiryo'
le after instrument mistaken for agent: 'card le' = by card not 'card paid'
colloquial merger: 'usle' = 'usley' (he/she did it)

title: Particle: bata
content:
Particle: bata (बाट)
Role: ablative / source / origin marker
Transaction direction: money_in_or_goods_in_from_source
Direction vector: INBOUND_SOURCE
Signal strength: HIGH
Variants: bata, baata, bāṭa, baat, bat
Disambiguation:
  • bata + aayo/paayo = inbound receipt
  • bata + jhikeko = withdrawal from named source
  • bata + kineko = purchase (source = supplier)
  • bata + kateko = deduction (negative from named source)
  • bata marks source; opposite direction to lai
Confusions:
  • bata vs dekhi: bata = physical source; dekhi = time-from or range-start
  • baata/bat are common short spellings
  • 'usle bata leko' = took from him (bata = source, le = agent)
Patterns:
- X bata paisa aayo → money came from X [inbound_receipt_from_named_source]
- bank bata jhikeko → withdrew from bank [withdrawal]
- X bata kineko → bought from X [purchase_from_named_supplier]
- X bata udharo leko → took credit from X [credit_received]
- salary bata kateko → deducted from salary [deduction_from_source]

disambiguation_rules: bata + aayo/paayo = inbound receipt
bata + jhikeko = withdrawal from named source
bata + kineko = purchase (source = supplier)
bata + kateko = deduction (negative from named source)
bata marks source; opposite direction to lai
common_confusions: bata vs dekhi: bata = physical source; dekhi = time-from or range-start
baata/bat are common short spellings
'usle bata leko' = took from him (bata = source, le = agent)

title: Particle: sanga
content:
Particle: sanga (सँग / संग)
Role: comitative / associative / possession marker
Transaction direction: party_holding_balance_or_joint_transaction
Direction vector: BILATERAL_OR_HOLDER
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: sanga, saanga, sāṃga, sita, sitaa
Disambiguation:
  • sanga + hisab = account reconciliation signal
  • sanga + baki/baaki = outstanding amount held by that party
  • sanga can mean WITH (joint) or FROM (informal purchase)
  • X sanga paise chha: X currently holds money (not necessarily owes)
  • saadh sanga = 'with' in settlement context
Confusions:
  • sanga vs bata for purchases: 'Ram sanga kineko' (informal) = 'Ram bata kineko' (formal)
  • sita is high-register synonym of sanga
  • sanga misread as possessive: 'mero sanga' = I have (possession, not transfer)
Patterns:
- X sanga hisab garnu → settle accounts with X [reconciliation_with_party]
- X sanga paise chha → money is with X / X holds balance [balance_held_by_party]
- X sanga kineko → bought from X (informal) [purchase_from_party]
- X sanga baki cha → balance outstanding with X [outstanding_balance_with_party]
- X sanga saadh gareko → settled with X [settlement_with_party]

example_patterns.meaning: money is with X / X holds balance

disambiguation_rules: sanga + hisab = account reconciliation signal
sanga + baki/baaki = outstanding amount held by that party
sanga can mean WITH (joint) or FROM (informal purchase)
X sanga paise chha: X currently holds money (not necessarily owes)
saadh sanga = 'with' in settlement context
common_confusions: sanga vs bata for purchases: 'Ram sanga kineko' (informal) = 'Ram bata kineko' (formal)
sita is high-register synonym of sanga
sanga misread as possessive: 'mero sanga' = I have (possession, not transfer)

title: Particle: ko
content:
Particle: ko (को)
Role: genitive / possessive / topic-of marker
Transaction direction: ownership_or_subject_of_transaction
Direction vector: OWNERSHIP_SCOPE
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: ko, kō, k0
Disambiguation:
  • ko before bill/hisab/baki = subject who owes or is owed
  • ko alone does not imply direction — needs verb for direction
  • person ko baki = that person's outstanding (receivable for you)
  • period ko hisab = date-scoped transaction filter
  • ko + amount = 'worth/of amount': '5000 ko maal' = goods worth 5000
Confusions:
  • ko vs lai: 'Ram ko bill' = Ram's bill; 'Ram lai bill' = give bill to Ram
  • '5000 ko' means 'worth 5000' not 'from 5000'
  • ko can nominalise: 'becheko ko paisa' = money from what was sold
Patterns:
- X ko paisa → X's money / money belonging to X [ownership_of_funds]
- X ko bill → X's bill / bill for X [liability_of_named_party]
- mahina ko hisab → monthly account [period_scoped_account]
- X ko baki → X's outstanding balance [receivable_from_X]
- Y ko lagi X tireko → paid X for Y [payment_purpose_marker]

example_patterns.meaning: X's money / money belonging to X

disambiguation_rules: ko before bill/hisab/baki = subject who owes or is owed
ko alone does not imply direction — needs verb for direction
person ko baki = that person's outstanding (receivable for you)
period ko hisab = date-scoped transaction filter
ko + amount = 'worth/of amount': '5000 ko maal' = goods worth 5000
common_confusions: ko vs lai: 'Ram ko bill' = Ram's bill; 'Ram lai bill' = give bill to Ram
'5000 ko' means 'worth 5000' not 'from 5000'
ko can nominalise: 'becheko ko paisa' = money from what was sold

title: Particle: ma
content:
Particle: ma (मा)
Role: locative / temporal / target marker
Transaction direction: location_of_transaction_or_deposit_target
Direction vector: LOCATION_OR_TARGET
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: ma, maa, mā, m
Disambiguation:
  • ma + jamma/haalnu = deposit target (inbound to that account/place)
  • ma + becheko = sale location (not direction of money)
  • ma + date = temporal anchor for transaction
  • account ma = transfer to account (inbound to account)
  • ma alone is neutral — needs verb to determine direction
Confusions:
  • ma vs lai for accounts: 'account ma' vs 'account lai' both used for deposit
  • temporal ma: 'June ma' = in June (not a place)
  • 'kharcha ma' = in expenses (categorisation, not location)
Patterns:
- bank ma jamma gareko → deposited in bank [deposit_to_location]
- account ma haaldeko → entered in account / transferred to account [account_credit]
- aaja ma tirne → will pay today [payment_on_date]
- pasal ma becheko → sold in/at shop [sale_at_location]
- site ma kharcha gareko → spent at site [expense_at_location]

example_patterns.meaning: entered in account / transferred to account

disambiguation_rules: ma + jamma/haalnu = deposit target (inbound to that account/place)
ma + becheko = sale location (not direction of money)
ma + date = temporal anchor for transaction
account ma = transfer to account (inbound to account)
ma alone is neutral — needs verb to determine direction
common_confusions: ma vs lai for accounts: 'account ma' vs 'account lai' both used for deposit
temporal ma: 'June ma' = in June (not a place)
'kharcha ma' = in expenses (categorisation, not location)

title: Particle: dekhi
content:
Particle: dekhi (देखि)
Role: ablative-temporal / range-start marker
Transaction direction: start_of_time_period_or_range_for_transaction_filter
Direction vector: TEMPORAL_RANGE_START
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: dekhi, deki, dekhī
Disambiguation:
  • dekhi = FROM (time); bata = FROM (place/source)
  • dekhi always marks the start of a range, not a source of funds
  • dekhi + date + samma = full time range for reports
  • dekhi alone can signal overdue calculation start
Confusions:
  • dekhi vs bata: 'bank bata' = from bank (source); 'Falgun dekhi' = from Falgun (time)
  • dekhera (from dekhnu = to see) is unrelated homophone
  • deki is common misspelling but same meaning
Patterns:
- Shrawan dekhi account hisab → account from Shrawan onwards [date_range_start_for_report]
- X bata dekhi payment aayena → no payment has come from X since [overdue_since_date]
- aaja dekhi udharo band → credit stopped from today [credit_freeze_from_date]
- 1 gate dekhi hisab nikaal → generate account from the 1st [report_period_start]
- yahi din dekhi baki rakheko → outstanding since this date [overdue_start_date]

example_patterns.meaning: no payment has come from X since

disambiguation_rules: dekhi = FROM (time); bata = FROM (place/source)
dekhi always marks the start of a range, not a source of funds
dekhi + date + samma = full time range for reports
dekhi alone can signal overdue calculation start
common_confusions: dekhi vs bata: 'bank bata' = from bank (source); 'Falgun dekhi' = from Falgun (time)
dekhera (from dekhnu = to see) is unrelated homophone
deki is common misspelling but same meaning

title: Particle: samma
content:
Particle: samma (सम्म)
Role: terminative / limit marker (until / up to / as far as)
Transaction direction: end_of_period_or_amount_ceiling_for_transaction
Direction vector: TEMPORAL_OR_AMOUNT_CEILING
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: samma, samm, sam, sammaa, sansamma
Disambiguation:
  • samma + date = deadline or reporting end date
  • samma + amount = ceiling/limit on credit or spending
  • dekhi...samma = from...to (range) for period reports
  • aaja samma = as of today (used in outstanding calculations)
  • samma does not imply direction — context verb required
Confusions:
  • samm/sam are colloquial short forms
  • 'samma' after amount = 'up to that amount' (ceiling, not total)
  • sometimes used loosely as 'about': '5000 samma' ≈ 'around 5000'
Patterns:
- Asar mash samma tirnu → must pay until Asar month [payment_deadline]
- 50000 samma udharo dinu → give credit up to 50000 [credit_limit]
- aaja samma baki cha → outstanding until today [overdue_as_of_today]
- Chaitra samma hisab banda → accounts closed until Chaitra [period_end_marker]
- kati samma dinu? → up to how much to give? [credit_limit_query]

metadata.grammatical_role: terminative / limit marker (until / up to / as far as)

example_patterns.meaning: up to how much to give?

disambiguation_rules: samma + date = deadline or reporting end date
samma + amount = ceiling/limit on credit or spending
dekhi...samma = from...to (range) for period reports
aaja samma = as of today (used in outstanding calculations)
samma does not imply direction — context verb required
common_confusions: samm/sam are colloquial short forms
'samma' after amount = 'up to that amount' (ceiling, not total)
sometimes used loosely as 'about': '5000 samma' ≈ 'around 5000'

title: Particle: lagi
content:
Particle: lagi (लागि)
Role: purposive / benefactive marker (for / in order to)
Transaction direction: purpose_or_beneficiary_of_transaction
Direction vector: PURPOSE_OR_BENEFICIARY
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: lagi, laagi, lāgi, lagī
Disambiguation:
  • lagi = 'for purpose of'; not same as lai (recipient)
  • lagi after project/reason = expense categorisation signal
  • X lagi = for X's benefit (beneficiary, not recipient of cash)
  • lagi + advance = purpose of the advance payment
  • lagi vs lai: 'Ram lagi' = for Ram's benefit; 'Ram lai' = to Ram directly
Confusions:
  • lagi and lai both translate as 'for/to' in English
  • 'mero lagi' = for me (purpose); 'malai' = to me (recipient)
  • laagi is extended form, same meaning
Patterns:
- school lagi fee tireko → paid fee for school [payment_purpose_labelled]
- construction lagi material kineko → bought material for construction [purchase_for_project]
- X lagi advance deko → gave advance for X [advance_for_purpose]
- rent lagi paisa rakheko → kept money for rent [fund_allocation]
- export lagi maal tayaar → goods ready for export [goods_purpose_tagged]

metadata.grammatical_role: purposive / benefactive marker (for / in order to)
disambiguation_rules: lagi = 'for purpose of'; not same as lai (recipient)
lagi after project/reason = expense categorisation signal
X lagi = for X's benefit (beneficiary, not recipient of cash)
lagi + advance = purpose of the advance payment
lagi vs lai: 'Ram lagi' = for Ram's benefit; 'Ram lai' = to Ram directly
common_confusions: lagi and lai both translate as 'for/to' in English
'mero lagi' = for me (purpose); 'malai' = to me (recipient)
laagi is extended form, same meaning

title: Particle: prati
content:
Particle: prati (प्रति)
Role: per / rate marker / towards (formal)
Transaction direction: unit_rate_or_formal_addressee
Direction vector: UNIT_RATE_OR_FORMAL_ADDRESSEE
Signal strength: HIGH_FOR_RATE
Variants: prati, priti, parti
Disambiguation:
  • prati + unit = per-unit rate calculation signal
  • prati + time = periodic rate (rent, salary, EMI)
  • prati + person (formal) = addressee of formal document
  • prati alone before amount without unit = formal 'towards'
  • rate context: prati is strong signal to extract unit price
Confusions:
  • prati vs lai for formal letters: both used as 'to'
  • priti is colloquial mispronunciation
  • 'parti' spelling common in typed Romanized Nepali
Patterns:
- prati kilo 120 rupiya → 120 rupees per kilogram [unit_rate_pricing]
- prati mahina 5000 → 5000 per month [periodic_payment_rate]
- prati share ek saya → 100 per share [per_unit_rate]
- X prati letter pathaaeko → sent letter to X (formal) [formal_correspondence_to_party]
- prati din ko hisab → per day account [daily_rate_calculation]

disambiguation_rules: prati + unit = per-unit rate calculation signal
prati + time = periodic rate (rent, salary, EMI)
prati + person (formal) = addressee of formal document
prati alone before amount without unit = formal 'towards'
rate context: prati is strong signal to extract unit price
common_confusions: prati vs lai for formal letters: both used as 'to'
priti is colloquial mispronunciation
'parti' spelling common in typed Romanized Nepali

title: Particle: bina
content:
Particle: bina (बिना)
Role: abessive / without marker
Transaction direction: absent_element_in_transaction
Direction vector: ABSENT_ELEMENT
Signal strength: HIGH_FOR_COMPLIANCE
Variants: bina, binna, vina, binaa
Disambiguation:
  • bina = without; flags missing document or step in transaction
  • bina + bill/receipt = undocumented transaction (compliance risk)
  • bina + approval = unauthorized transaction signal
  • bina is a negative dependency marker — what was absent
  • critical for audit/compliance detection in transaction logs
Confusions:
  • bina vs bahek: bina = without (absent element); bahek = except (excluded member of set)
  • vina is formal Sanskrit variant, same meaning
  • binna is a frequent misspelling
Patterns:
- bill bina payment gareko → paid without a bill [informal_unreceipted_payment]
- receipt bina maal deko → gave goods without receipt [undocumented_sale]
- anumati bina nikasaa → withdrawal without approval [unauthorized_withdrawal]
- contract bina kaam gareko → worked without contract [informal_uncontracted_work]
- hisab bina tireko → paid without accounting [unrecorded_payment]

disambiguation_rules: bina = without; flags missing document or step in transaction
bina + bill/receipt = undocumented transaction (compliance risk)
bina + approval = unauthorized transaction signal
bina is a negative dependency marker — what was absent
critical for audit/compliance detection in transaction logs
common_confusions: bina vs bahek: bina = without (absent element); bahek = except (excluded member of set)
vina is formal Sanskrit variant, same meaning
binna is a frequent misspelling

title: Particle: bahek
content:
Particle: bahek (बाहेक)
Role: exceptive / exclusion marker (except / other than)
Transaction direction: excluded_party_or_item_from_transaction
Direction vector: EXCLUSION
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: bahek, baahek, baheka, baaheek
Disambiguation:
  • bahek = except; marks what is NOT included in the transaction/set
  • VAT bahek = exclusive of tax (important for amount parsing)
  • bahek + party = excluded payer/payee
  • bahek is set-exclusion; bina is element-absence — different semantics
  • use bahek to identify carve-outs from totals
Confusions:
  • bahek vs bina: 'receipt bina' = without receipt; 'Ram bahek' = except Ram
  • baahek extended spelling same meaning
  • colloquially sometimes used as 'other than' with loose logic
Patterns:
- Ram bahek sabai le tireka → everyone except Ram has paid [exclusion_from_payers_list]
- VAT bahek ko amount → amount excluding VAT [tax_exclusive_amount]
- advance bahek baki tirnu → pay balance except advance [net_after_exclusion]
- is mahina bahek sabai clear → all clear except this month [period_exclusion]
- X bahek sabai items stock ma → all items in stock except X [item_exclusion_from_inventory]

example_patterns.pattern: X bahek sabai items stock ma
example_patterns.meaning: all items in stock except X

disambiguation_rules: bahek = except; marks what is NOT included in the transaction/set
VAT bahek = exclusive of tax (important for amount parsing)
bahek + party = excluded payer/payee
bahek is set-exclusion; bina is element-absence — different semantics
use bahek to identify carve-outs from totals
common_confusions: bahek vs bina: 'receipt bina' = without receipt; 'Ram bahek' = except Ram
baahek extended spelling same meaning
colloquially sometimes used as 'other than' with loose logic

title: Particle: bhanda
content:
Particle: bhanda (भन्दा)
Role: comparative marker (than / more than / less than)
Transaction direction: comparison_of_amounts_or_parties
Direction vector: COMPARATIVE_THRESHOLD
Signal strength: HIGH_FOR_FILTERS
Variants: bhanda, vanda, bhannda, bhanta, bhandaa
Disambiguation:
  • bhanda + badi/beshi = above threshold (filter signal)
  • bhanda + ghati/kami = below threshold
  • bhanda is always comparative; not a transfer direction marker
  • bhanda pairs with badi/ghati/beshi/kami to form comparisons
  • use bhanda to detect threshold filters in queries: 'show me amounts > X'
Confusions:
  • vanda is colloquial Romanization
  • bhanta is misspelling
  • bhanda (than) vs bhannu (to say) — different words, same root confusion in text
Patterns:
- 100 bhanda badi → more than 100 [amount_above_threshold]
- last mahina bhanda ghati bikri → lower sales than last month [comparative_performance]
- X bhanda Y sasta → Y cheaper than X [price_comparison]
- expected bhanda badi kharcha → expenses more than expected [budget_overrun]
- budget bhanda kam kharcheko → spent less than budget [budget_underspend]

metadata.grammatical_role: comparative marker (than / more than / less than)
disambiguation_rules: bhanda + badi/beshi = above threshold (filter signal)
bhanda + ghati/kami = below threshold
bhanda is always comparative; not a transfer direction marker
bhanda pairs with badi/ghati/beshi/kami to form comparisons
use bhanda to detect threshold filters in queries: 'show me amounts > X'
common_confusions: vanda is colloquial Romanization
bhanta is misspelling
bhanda (than) vs bhannu (to say) — different words, same root confusion in text

title: Particle: jasto
content:
Particle: jasto (जस्तो)
Role: similative / approximative marker (like / approximately)
Transaction direction: approximate_amount_or_analogy
Direction vector: APPROXIMATION
Signal strength: LOW_APPROXIMATE
Variants: jasto, jestho, jasstho, jsto
Disambiguation:
  • jasto + amount = treat as approximate, not exact figure
  • jasto = similarity hedge; reduces confidence in exact value extraction
  • jasto chhaina = doesn't match (reconciliation mismatch signal)
  • lagyo + jasto = epistemic uncertainty ('seems like')
  • parser should flag jasto-modified amounts as approximate
Confusions:
  • jasto vs jati: jasto = like/similar; jati = as much as
  • jestho is a misspelling (also homophone of 'elder' in some dialects)
  • jasto chhaina ≠ not like; context-dependent negation
Patterns:
- 5000 jasto ho → it is approximately 5000 [approximate_amount]
- last time jasto nai payment → payment similar to last time [reference_to_prior_transaction]
- salary jasto paisa → money like salary / salary-like income [income_category_approximation]
- hisab mileko jasto chhaina → accounts don't seem to match [reconciliation_doubt]
- becheko jasto lagyo → it seems like it was sold [uncertain_sale_detection]

example_patterns.meaning: it seems like it was sold

disambiguation_rules: jasto + amount = treat as approximate, not exact figure
jasto = similarity hedge; reduces confidence in exact value extraction
jasto chhaina = doesn't match (reconciliation mismatch signal)
lagyo + jasto = epistemic uncertainty ('seems like')
parser should flag jasto-modified amounts as approximate
common_confusions: jasto vs jati: jasto = like/similar; jati = as much as
jestho is a misspelling (also homophone of 'elder' in some dialects)
jasto chhaina ≠ not like; context-dependent negation

title: Particle: pachhadi
content:
Particle: pachhadi (पछाडि)
Role: temporal/spatial postposition (after / behind)
Transaction direction: transaction_occurring_after_event_or_deadline
Direction vector: TEMPORAL_AFTER
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: pachhadi, pachadi, pachaadi, pachi, pachhi
Disambiguation:
  • pachhadi + event = payment/action contingent on prior event
  • pachhadi + deadline = late payment indicator
  • pachhadi is temporal sequencing — does not mark direction of money
  • pachi is short form, same meaning
  • pair with verb to determine if payment is late or conditional
Confusions:
  • pachhadi vs agaadi: pachhadi = after/behind; agaadi = before/ahead
  • pachi is very common short form
  • pachhi also used — same meaning
Patterns:
- maal aayepachi payment → payment after goods arrive [deferred_payment_on_delivery]
- audit pachhadi hisab garnu → account after audit [post_audit_settlement]
- deadline pachhadi tireko → paid after deadline [late_payment]
- contract pachhadi advance dinchhu → will give advance after contract [conditional_future_payment]
- approval pachhadi release garnu → release after approval [conditional_fund_release]

disambiguation_rules: pachhadi + event = payment/action contingent on prior event
pachhadi + deadline = late payment indicator
pachhadi is temporal sequencing — does not mark direction of money
pachi is short form, same meaning
pair with verb to determine if payment is late or conditional
common_confusions: pachhadi vs agaadi: pachhadi = after/behind; agaadi = before/ahead
pachi is very common short form
pachhi also used — same meaning

title: Particle: agaadi
content:
Particle: agaadi (अगाडि)
Role: temporal/spatial postposition (before / ahead / in front)
Transaction direction: advance_payment_or_precondition_before_transaction
Direction vector: TEMPORAL_BEFORE_OR_ADVANCE
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: agaadi, agadi, agaade, aghi, pehile
Disambiguation:
  • agaadi + payment = advance or prepayment
  • agaadi marks precondition — action before another event
  • agaadi vs pachhadi: agaadi = before; pachhadi = after
  • aghi is shorter near-synonym, very common in speech
  • pehile is another synonym (before, earlier)
Confusions:
  • agaadi vs aghi: same meaning, aghi more colloquial
  • pehile preferred in temporal 'earlier' sense
  • agaadi can be spatial (in front of) or temporal (before)
Patterns:
- kaam agaadi advance dinu → give advance before work starts [advance_payment]
- delivery agaadi payment garnuhos → pay before delivery [prepayment_condition]
- contract agaadi hisab garnu → settle accounts before contract [pre_contract_settlement]
- tax deadline agaadi tirnu → pay before tax deadline [pre_deadline_payment]
- agaadi payment gareko cha → payment has been made in advance [advance_payment_confirmed]

example_patterns.meaning: payment has been made in advance

disambiguation_rules: agaadi + payment = advance or prepayment
agaadi marks precondition — action before another event
agaadi vs pachhadi: agaadi = before; pachhadi = after
aghi is shorter near-synonym, very common in speech
pehile is another synonym (before, earlier)
common_confusions: agaadi vs aghi: same meaning, aghi more colloquial
pehile preferred in temporal 'earlier' sense
agaadi can be spatial (in front of) or temporal (before)

title: Particle: mathi
content:
Particle: mathi (माथि)
Role: locative / amount threshold (above / over / on top)
Transaction direction: amount_above_threshold_or_additional_charge
Direction vector: ABOVE_THRESHOLD_OR_ADDITIONAL
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: mathi, maathi, maathhi, mati, mathii
Disambiguation:
  • mathi + amount = above that amount (filter threshold)
  • mathi = additional/on top of (stacking charges)
  • mathi bata = from above (hierarchy, not amount)
  • amount mathi = exceeds amount (overrun signal)
  • mathi vs tala: mathi = above/over; tala = below/under
Confusions:
  • mati is frequent misspelling (also means 'soil' in some dialects)
  • mathi (above) vs mathi (head office direction) — context dependent
  • spatial vs amount sense must be disambiguated by context
Patterns:
- 10000 mathi ko transaction → transactions above 10000 [above_threshold_filter]
- basic salary mathi bonus → bonus on top of basic salary [additional_amount_over_base]
- mathi bata order aayo → order came from above (head office) [instruction_from_higher_authority]
- rate mathi bechhnu hundaina → cannot sell above rate [price_ceiling]
- budget mathi kharcha bhayo → expenditure went above budget [budget_overrun]

metadata.grammatical_role: locative / amount threshold (above / over / on top)

example_patterns.meaning: bonus on top of basic salary

disambiguation_rules: mathi + amount = above that amount (filter threshold)
mathi = additional/on top of (stacking charges)
mathi bata = from above (hierarchy, not amount)
amount mathi = exceeds amount (overrun signal)
mathi vs tala: mathi = above/over; tala = below/under
common_confusions: mati is frequent misspelling (also means 'soil' in some dialects)
mathi (above) vs mathi (head office direction) — context dependent
spatial vs amount sense must be disambiguated by context

title: Particle: tala
content:
Particle: tala (तल)
Role: locative / amount threshold (below / under)
Transaction direction: amount_below_threshold_or_subordinate_level
Direction vector: BELOW_THRESHOLD
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: tala, talla, taala, tal, tla
Disambiguation:
  • tala + amount = below that amount (lower bound filter)
  • tala = under/below in both spatial and amount senses
  • tala bata = from below (branch/subordinate)
  • tala + stock = reorder alert signal
  • tala vs mathi: tala = below; mathi = above
Confusions:
  • tal is short form — same meaning
  • tala (below) vs tala (floor/lock) — homophones in different contexts
  • taala means 'lock' — do not confuse with tala (below)
Patterns:
- 5000 tala ko bikri → sales below 5000 [below_threshold_filter]
- tala ko branch bata report → report from subordinate branch [sub_branch_reporting]
- minimum tala gayo stock → stock went below minimum [stock_below_reorder_level]
- rate tala aaeko cha → rate has come down [price_decrease]
- tala tirnu bhayena → couldn't pay below (minimum payment) [minimum_payment_missed]

disambiguation_rules: tala + amount = below that amount (lower bound filter)
tala = under/below in both spatial and amount senses
tala bata = from below (branch/subordinate)
tala + stock = reorder alert signal
tala vs mathi: tala = below; mathi = above
common_confusions: tal is short form — same meaning
tala (below) vs tala (floor/lock) — homophones in different contexts
taala means 'lock' — do not confuse with tala (below)

title: Particle: bhitra
content:
Particle: bhitra (भित्र)
Role: interior locative / inclusional (inside / within)
Transaction direction: within_time_period_or_within_scope_of_transaction
Direction vector: WITHIN_SCOPE_OR_DEADLINE
Signal strength: HIGH_FOR_DEADLINE
Variants: bhitra, bhitri, vitara, bitra, bhitir
Disambiguation:
  • bhitra + time = deadline (must complete within period)
  • bhitra + amount = within budget (spending constraint)
  • bhitra + location = geographic delivery scope
  • bhitra is always inclusional — inside a boundary
  • bhitra vs baahira: bhitra = within; baahira = outside
Confusions:
  • bitra is common misspelling
  • bhitri = interior (adjective form, not postposition)
  • bhitra (within) vs bhitra (inside building) — same word, context determines scope
Patterns:
- 7 din bhitra tirnu parcha → must pay within 7 days [payment_within_deadline]
- budget bhitra kharcha garnu → spend within budget [within_budget_constraint]
- Kathmandu bhitra delivery → delivery within Kathmandu [delivery_zone_scope]
- contract bhitra kaam saknu → complete work within contract [scope_of_contract]
- scope bhitra matra billing → billing only within scope [billing_scope_constraint]

disambiguation_rules: bhitra + time = deadline (must complete within period)
bhitra + amount = within budget (spending constraint)
bhitra + location = geographic delivery scope
bhitra is always inclusional — inside a boundary
bhitra vs baahira: bhitra = within; baahira = outside
common_confusions: bitra is common misspelling
bhitri = interior (adjective form, not postposition)
bhitra (within) vs bhitra (inside building) — same word, context determines scope

title: Particle: baahira
content:
Particle: baahira (बाहिर)
Role: exterior locative / exclusional (outside / beyond)
Transaction direction: outside_scope_additional_charge_or_outstation
Direction vector: OUTSIDE_SCOPE_OR_ZONE
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: baahira, bahira, bahire, baahire, baahir
Disambiguation:
  • baahira + location = out-of-zone (surcharge possible)
  • baahira + budget = overspend signal
  • baahira + scope = out-of-scope (change order needed)
  • Nepal baahira = international transaction / export
  • baahira vs bhitra: baahira = outside boundary; bhitra = inside boundary
Confusions:
  • bahira is common short spelling
  • baahira (outside) vs baahira (going out) — same root
  • VAT baahira = VAT exempt, not 'sent outside for VAT'
Patterns:
- Kathmandu baahira delivery charge alag → extra charge for delivery outside Kathmandu [out_of_zone_surcharge]
- budget baahira kharcha bhayo → expenditure went outside budget [over_budget_expenditure]
- contract baahira kaam gareko → worked outside contract scope [out_of_scope_work]
- Nepal baahira pathaaeko → sent outside Nepal [international_transfer_or_export]
- VAT baahira ko item → item outside VAT scope [vat_exempt_item]

example_patterns.meaning: extra charge for delivery outside Kathmandu

disambiguation_rules: baahira + location = out-of-zone (surcharge possible)
baahira + budget = overspend signal
baahira + scope = out-of-scope (change order needed)
Nepal baahira = international transaction / export
baahira vs bhitra: baahira = outside boundary; bhitra = inside boundary
common_confusions: bahira is common short spelling
baahira (outside) vs baahira (going out) — same root
VAT baahira = VAT exempt, not 'sent outside for VAT'

title: Particle: nira
content:
Particle: nira (नेर / नीर)
Role: proximal locative (near / close to)
Transaction direction: proximity_indicator_rarely_used_in_transaction_context
Direction vector: LOCATION_PROXIMITY
Signal strength: LOW
Variants: nira, nera, neera, neer
Disambiguation:
  • nira = near (spatial); almost never marks money direction
  • nira used in delivery address specification
  • nira is low-signal for financial transactions; flag as location only
  • nira vs najik: same meaning; najik more common in formal writing
  • parser: nira → extract location entity, not transaction direction
Confusions:
  • nira (near) vs nira (name) — common Nepali name
  • neer is Romanized misspelling
  • confuse with nira (name) in customer name fields
Patterns:
- bank nira pasal → shop near the bank [location_reference]
- mero ghar nira pathaaunu → send near my house / to my area [delivery_proximity]
- bazaar nira delivery dinus → deliver near the market [approximate_delivery_point]
- nira aako customer → customer who came nearby [walk_in_customer_context]
- office nira account khol → open account near office [location_preference]

example_patterns.meaning: send near my house / to my area

disambiguation_rules: nira = near (spatial); almost never marks money direction
nira used in delivery address specification
nira is low-signal for financial transactions; flag as location only
nira vs najik: same meaning; najik more common in formal writing
parser: nira → extract location entity, not transaction direction
common_confusions: nira (near) vs nira (name) — common Nepali name
neer is Romanized misspelling
confuse with nira (name) in customer name fields

title: Particle: tadha
content:
Particle: tadha (टाढा)
Role: distal locative (far / distant)
Transaction direction: distance_surcharge_or_outstation_indicator
Direction vector: DISTANCE_OR_REMOTE
Signal strength: LOW_EXCEPT_LOGISTICS
Variants: tadha, taadha, taadhaa, tadhaa, taaDha
Disambiguation:
  • tadha = far; implies higher logistics cost
  • tadha bata = from a distant source (freight cost signal)
  • tadha delivery = flag for distance-based pricing
  • tadha vs najik: tadha = far; najik = near
  • parser: tadha → flag for transport surcharge lookup
Confusions:
  • taadha is common extended spelling
  • taaDha is Unicode-transliteration form
  • tadha (far) unrelated to tadha in some dialects meaning 'then'
Patterns:
- tadha ko delivery charge badi → higher delivery charge for distant location [distance_based_surcharge]
- tadha bata aayeko maal → goods that came from far [long_distance_freight]
- tadha ko branch ko hisab → accounts of the distant branch [remote_branch_accounting]
- tadha tirna garo → hard to collect from far [collection_difficulty_indicator]
- tadha ko supplier bata kineko → bought from distant supplier [long_supply_chain_purchase]

example_patterns.meaning: higher delivery charge for distant location

disambiguation_rules: tadha = far; implies higher logistics cost
tadha bata = from a distant source (freight cost signal)
tadha delivery = flag for distance-based pricing
tadha vs najik: tadha = far; najik = near
parser: tadha → flag for transport surcharge lookup
common_confusions: taadha is common extended spelling
taaDha is Unicode-transliteration form
tadha (far) unrelated to tadha in some dialects meaning 'then'

title: Particle: bich
content:
Particle: bich (बीच)
Role: medial / between marker
Transaction direction: transaction_between_two_parties_or_within_range
Direction vector: INTER_PARTY_OR_RANGE
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: bich, beech, bichh, bicch, beec
Disambiguation:
  • bich = between two parties or two amounts
  • bich ma = in between / in the middle (middleman signal)
  • date bich = date range for filtering
  • bich ko hisab = inter-party account requiring two-sided entry
  • bich commission = intermediary fee
Confusions:
  • beech is English-influenced spelling
  • bich (between) vs beech (tree in English)
  • bichh/bicch are common typos
Patterns:
- Ram ra Shyam bich ko hisab → account between Ram and Shyam [inter_party_settlement]
- 1 bata 15 gate bich ko bikri → sales between 1st and 15th [date_range_filter]
- do company bich transaction → transaction between two companies [inter_company_transfer]
- 5000 ra 10000 bich ko amount → amount between 5000 and 10000 [amount_range_filter]
- bich ma commission leko → took commission in between / as middleman [middleman_commission]

example_patterns.pattern: Ram ra Shyam bich ko hisab

example_patterns.meaning: took commission in between / as middleman

disambiguation_rules: bich = between two parties or two amounts
bich ma = in between / in the middle (middleman signal)
date bich = date range for filtering
bich ko hisab = inter-party account requiring two-sided entry
bich commission = intermediary fee
common_confusions: beech is English-influenced spelling
bich (between) vs beech (tree in English)
bichh/bicch are common typos

title: Particle: wari
content:
Particle: wari (वारि)
Role: proximal side marker (this side / on our side)
Transaction direction: transaction_on_our_side_of_boundary_or_domestic
Direction vector: OWN_SIDE_DOMESTIC
Signal strength: LOW
Variants: wari, waari, vari, waare, wāri
Disambiguation:
  • wari = this side / our side (near side of boundary)
  • wari vs pari: wari = near side; pari = far/other side
  • wari bata = from our side (we are sending/giving)
  • wari ko = belonging to / on our side
  • in trade: wari = domestic/local; pari = across border
Confusions:
  • wari (this side) vs wari (regarding — archaic usage)
  • vari is alternate spelling
  • waari is extended Romanization
Patterns:
- wari ko hisab mildaina → our side's accounts don't match [domestic_side_reconciliation_issue]
- wari bata payment pathaaeko → payment sent from this side [payment_from_our_party]
- wari ko stock kati cha → how much stock is on our side [own_inventory_check]
- nadi wari ko delivery → delivery on this side of the river [local_zone_delivery]
- wari ma rakheko paisa → money held on our side [own_held_funds]

metadata.grammatical_role: proximal side marker (this side / on our side)

example_patterns.meaning: how much stock is on our side
delivery on this side of the river

disambiguation_rules: wari = this side / our side (near side of boundary)
wari vs pari: wari = near side; pari = far/other side
wari bata = from our side (we are sending/giving)
wari ko = belonging to / on our side
in trade: wari = domestic/local; pari = across border
common_confusions: wari (this side) vs wari (regarding — archaic usage)
vari is alternate spelling
waari is extended Romanization

title: Particle: pari
content:
Particle: pari (पारि)
Role: distal side marker (other side / across)
Transaction direction: transaction_across_boundary_or_counterparty_side
Direction vector: COUNTERPARTY_OR_CROSS_BORDER
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: pari, paari, pāri, pare, paare
Disambiguation:
  • pari = other side / counterparty
  • pari vs wari: pari = their side; wari = our side
  • pari bata = from the other party (inbound expected)
  • pari ko = belonging to counterparty / cross-border
  • pari + payment pending = accounts receivable from counterparty
Confusions:
  • pari (other side) vs pari (fairy — English loanword in Nepali)
  • paari is extended spelling
  • pari bata vs pari ko: source vs ownership distinction
Patterns:
- pari ko party bata payment aayena → payment has not come from the other party [counterparty_payment_pending]
- India pari ko supplier → supplier across in India [cross_border_purchase]
- pari ko account ma pathaaeko → sent to account on the other side [inter_bank_or_cross_border_transfer]
- pari ra wari ko hisab milaaunu → reconcile both sides of accounts [bilateral_reconciliation]
- pari le paisa pathaaeko chhaina → the other side hasn't sent money [counterparty_default]

example_patterns.pattern: pari ko party bata payment aayena
example_patterns.meaning: payment has not come from the other party
sent to account on the other side

example_patterns.pattern: pari ra wari ko hisab milaaunu

example_patterns.meaning: the other side hasn't sent money

disambiguation_rules: pari = other side / counterparty
pari vs wari: pari = their side; wari = our side
pari bata = from the other party (inbound expected)
pari ko = belonging to counterparty / cross-border
pari + payment pending = accounts receivable from counterparty
common_confusions: pari (other side) vs pari (fairy — English loanword in Nepali)
paari is extended spelling
pari bata vs pari ko: source vs ownership distinction

title: Particle: tira
content:
Particle: tira (तिर)
Role: directional / approximate direction marker (towards / around)
Transaction direction: approximate_direction_of_transaction_or_approximate_time
Direction vector: APPROXIMATE_DIRECTION
Signal strength: MEDIUM_APPROXIMATE
Variants: tira, tir, tiri, tiraa, tiir
Disambiguation:
  • tira = approximate direction (less precise than lai)
  • tira after person/place = towards, not definitive recipient
  • tira after time = approximately at that time
  • payment tira = payment direction (weaker than payment lai)
  • tira signals approximation — extract with lower confidence
Confusions:
  • tira (towards) vs tirnu (to pay) — different words, easy confusion in short text
  • tir is short form of tira (same meaning)
  • tira and lai sometimes interchanged: 'X tira deko' ≈ 'X lai deko'
Patterns:
- supplier tira payment gayo → payment went towards supplier [payment_directed_to_supplier]
- desh tira paisa pathaaeko → money sent towards home country (remittance) [remittance_outbound]
- sanjh tira bhuktaan garchhu → will settle around evening [approximate_time_of_payment]
- market tira rate ghate → rate decreased towards market (market direction) [market_rate_movement]
- bank tira janu parla → might need to go towards bank [deferred_banking_action]

example_patterns.meaning: might need to go towards bank

disambiguation_rules: tira = approximate direction (less precise than lai)
tira after person/place = towards, not definitive recipient
tira after time = approximately at that time
payment tira = payment direction (weaker than payment lai)
tira signals approximation — extract with lower confidence
common_confusions: tira (towards) vs tirnu (to pay) — different words, easy confusion in short text
tir is short form of tira (same meaning)
tira and lai sometimes interchanged: 'X tira deko' ≈ 'X lai deko'

title: Particle: najik
content:
Particle: najik (नजिक)
Role: proximal locative (near / close to)
Transaction direction: physical_proximity_for_delivery_or_service_location
Direction vector: PROXIMITY_OR_APPROACHING_DEADLINE
Signal strength: LOW_EXCEPT_DEADLINE
Variants: najik, najikk, najeek, najic, najīk
Disambiguation:
  • najik = near (spatial) or approaching (temporal)
  • najik + deadline = urgency signal for payment
  • najik ko branch = nearest service point
  • najik aako = approaching (temporal, deadline alert)
  • najik vs nira: both mean near; najik more formal/common
Confusions:
  • najik (near) used temporally: 'due date najik cha' = deadline approaching
  • najeek is Romanized colloquial form
  • najik aako payment = payment approaching (not payment nearby)
Patterns:
- najik ko branch ma tirnu → pay at nearest branch [payment_at_nearby_location]
- ghar najik delivery dinuhos → please deliver near my home [nearby_delivery]
- najik ko supplier bata kineko → bought from nearby supplier [local_purchase]
- najik aako payment → payment almost due / close to due date [payment_approaching_deadline]
- deadline najik cha → deadline is near [approaching_deadline_alert]

example_patterns.meaning: payment almost due / close to due date

disambiguation_rules: najik = near (spatial) or approaching (temporal)
najik + deadline = urgency signal for payment
najik ko branch = nearest service point
najik aako = approaching (temporal, deadline alert)
najik vs nira: both mean near; najik more formal/common
common_confusions: najik (near) used temporally: 'due date najik cha' = deadline approaching
najeek is Romanized colloquial form
najik aako payment = payment approaching (not payment nearby)

title: Particle: muni
content:
Particle: muni (मुनि)
Role: sub-locative (under / beneath / below authority)
Transaction direction: under_authority_or_below_category
Direction vector: UNDER_CATEGORY_OR_AUTHORITY
Signal strength: LOW
Variants: muni, munii, mooni, mune, munī
Disambiguation:
  • muni = under authority, not physical 'below'
  • muni + tax = category/classification signal
  • muni + budget = within a budget line item
  • muni + person = hierarchical subordination
  • muni vs tala: muni = under authority/category; tala = physically below or numerically lower
Confusions:
  • muni (under) vs Muni (sage/saint — proper noun)
  • mooni is colloquial Romanization
  • muni and tala both translate as 'under' but different uses
Patterns:
- VAT muni aaucha → falls under VAT [tax_category_assignment]
- budget muni rakhnu → keep under budget [budget_category_allocation]
- X ko muni kaam gareko → worked under X's authority [sub_contractor_labour]
- exemption muni parcha → falls under exemption [tax_exempt_classification]
- muni tirnu hunchha → payment comes under (this category) [categorised_payment]

disambiguation_rules: muni = under authority, not physical 'below'
muni + tax = category/classification signal
muni + budget = within a budget line item
muni + person = hierarchical subordination
muni vs tala: muni = under authority/category; tala = physically below or numerically lower
common_confusions: muni (under) vs Muni (sage/saint — proper noun)
mooni is colloquial Romanization
muni and tala both translate as 'under' but different uses

title: Particle: upara
content:
Particle: upara (उपर / माथि)
Role: super-locative / additional charge marker (over / upon / on top of)
Transaction direction: additional_charge_or_levy_on_top_of_base
Direction vector: ADDITIONAL_CHARGE_ON_BASE
Signal strength: HIGH_FOR_TAX_SURCHARGE
Variants: upara, upari, upar, upaar, upra
Disambiguation:
  • upara = on top of / over (stacking charges)
  • upara + percentage = tax/surcharge rate applied to base
  • udharo upara byaaj = compound interest signal
  • upara vs mathi: upara more formal/legal; mathi more colloquial
  • upara marks what is added ON TOP of a base figure
Confusions:
  • upara and mathi are near synonyms: upara more formal, mathi more conversational
  • upar is short form (North Indian influence)
  • upara (on top) vs upari (adjective: upper)
Patterns:
- base amount upara 13% VAT → 13% VAT on top of base amount [tax_on_base_amount]
- penalty upara surcharge → surcharge on top of penalty [compound_levy]
- udharo upara byaaj → interest on credit [interest_on_outstanding]
- total upara discount deko → gave discount on total [discount_on_base]
- upara utha aayo → came on top (surplus) [surplus_above_expected]

metadata.grammatical_role: super-locative / additional charge marker (over / upon / on top of)

example_patterns.meaning: 13% VAT on top of base amount

disambiguation_rules: upara = on top of / over (stacking charges)
upara + percentage = tax/surcharge rate applied to base
udharo upara byaaj = compound interest signal
upara vs mathi: upara more formal/legal; mathi more colloquial
upara marks what is added ON TOP of a base figure
common_confusions: upara and mathi are near synonyms: upara more formal, mathi more conversational
upar is short form (North Indian influence)
upara (on top) vs upari (adjective: upper)

title: Particle: aghi
content:
Particle: aghi (अघि)
Role: temporal antecedent (before / earlier / prior)
Transaction direction: prior_to_event_or_earlier_payment_reference
Direction vector: PRIOR_HISTORICAL_REFERENCE
Signal strength: MEDIUM
Variants: aghi, agee, agi, aghee, aghai
Disambiguation:
  • aghi = prior to / earlier than (temporal only)
  • aghi ko = previous / prior (references historical record)
  • aghi deko = given previously (look up prior entry)
  • aghi vs agaadi: aghi = prior (already happened); agaadi = ahead (hasn't happened)
  • aghi signals need to look up historical transaction records
Confusions:
  • aghi vs agaadi: aghi = earlier (past reference); agaadi = in front / before (future or spatial)
  • agi is common misspelling
  • aghai is emphatic form: 'aghai deko thiyo' = had given even earlier
Patterns:
- aghi deko advance → advance given earlier [prior_advance_reference]
- aghi ko hisab baki cha → earlier accounts are still outstanding [historical_outstanding]
- aghi tireko payment → payment made earlier/previously [prior_payment_reference]
- aghi milaaeko rate → rate agreed earlier [pre_agreed_rate]
- yesaghi khasee aayena → hadn't come/paid before this [first_time_payment_or_default]

disambiguation_rules: aghi = prior to / earlier than (temporal only)
aghi ko = previous / prior (references historical record)
aghi deko = given previously (look up prior entry)
aghi vs agaadi: aghi = prior (already happened); agaadi = ahead (hasn't happened)
aghi signals need to look up historical transaction records
common_confusions: aghi vs agaadi: aghi = earlier (past reference); agaadi = in front / before (future or spatial)
agi is common misspelling
aghai is emphatic form: 'aghai deko thiyo' = had given even earlier
